title: erianthus repens
intro: Erianthus repens, also known as Crow Poison or False Brome, is a perennial grass native to Europe, western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Erianthus repens include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health. Erianthus repens can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for various herbal preparations to be utilized for medicinal purposes.
